purpose: Reduce the LiteDRAM open P&R failure to minimal ODELAYE2 output-buffer topologies.

cases.name: ODELAYE2 to OBUF
cases.error: ODELAYE2 'odelay' has DATAOUT connected to unsupported cell type IOB33_OUTBUF

cases.name: ODELAYE2 to OBUFDS
cases.error: ODELAYE2 'odelay' has DATAOUT connected to unsupported cell type IOB33M_OUTBUF

patched_nextpnr_result.purpose: Allow the packer to accept ODELAYE2 DATAOUT users of type IOB33_OUTBUF or IOB33M_OUTBUF so the cutout can reach the next architectural check.

cases.name: ODELAYE2 to OBUF with patched nextpnr
cases.error: BEL IOB_X0Y92/IOB33/OUTBUF is located on a high range bank. High range banks do not have ODELAY

cases.name: ODELAYE2 to OBUFDS with patched nextpnr
cases.error: BEL IOB_X0Y94/IOB33M/OUTBUF is located on a high range bank. High range banks do not have ODELAY

patched_nextpnr_result.inference: The original unsupported-cell error was a narrower nextpnr packer acceptance gap. Once patched, the cutouts expose the board-relevant architectural blocker: these HR-bank IOB33 sites do not provide output ODELAY. The next open path is a LiteDRAM no-ODELAY Series-7 PHY variant, not Vivado MIG.

durable_json_policy: Generated cutout JSON netlists are not copied into the repository because each is about 9.7 MiB; regenerate them with the recorded flake JSON targets when needed.
inference: The LiteDRAM probe P&R blocker is reproduced by a one-ODELAY cutout. A small nextpnr patch gets past the first unsupported-cell check, but the next failure reports that the constrained HR-bank IOB33 sites have no ODELAY. It is not explained by TinyStories, the full controller size, or the no-iopad versus iopad synthesis choice.
